LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION OF THE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR PREMISES LIABILITY AGAINST THE CITY IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FACE OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT SHOWS THAT THE SOLE CLAIM AGAINST THE CITY WAS FILED MORE THAN SIX MONTHS AFTER THE CITY DENIED MR. JUAREZ'S GOVERNMENT CODE CLAIM. MR. JUAREZ HAS NOT ALLEGED SUFFICIENT FACTS, NOR DOES IT APPEAR THAT HE REASONABLY COULD ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS, THAT HIS CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THE CITY RELATES BACK TO THE FILING OF THE COMPLAINT OR THAT EITHER EQUITABLE TOLLING OR EQUITABLE ESTOPPEL APPLIES. THE RELATION BACK DOCTRINE IS NOT APPLICABLE SINCE MR. JUAREZ'S GOVERNMENT CODE CLAIM SHOWS THAT HE KNEW ABOUT THE CITY'S POSSIBLE LIABILITY BEFORE FILING HIS COMPLAINT AND THUS MR. JUAREZ CANNOT AVAIL HIMSELF OF THE CCP 474 PROCEDURES. EQUITABLE TOLLING IS INAPPLICABLE SINCE THERE WAS NO PRIOR LAWSUIT WHICH TOLLS THE RUNNING OF ANY TIME PERIOD IN THIS LAWSUIT. EQUITABLE ESTOPPEL IS INAPPLICABLE SINCE THE CITY'S DENIAL OF THE GOVERNMENT CODE CLAIM DID NOT MAKE ANY MISLEADING STATEMENTS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Jun-29-2016 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-09-2016 Continued To Jun-29-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Geraldine Armendariz of Law Office of Geraldine Armendariz telephone number 415-986-0873 for Plaintiff Jeffrey Juarez.
Diana Rosenstein of The Office of The City Attorney telephone number 415-554-3933 for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO the second cause of action of the 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT for premises liability against the City is sustained without leave to amend. The face of the second amended complaint shows that the sole claim against the City was filed more than six months after the City denied Mr. Juarez's Government Code claim. Mr. Juarez has not alleged sufficient facts, nor does it appear that he reasonably could allege sufficient facts, that his cause of action against the City relates back to the filing of the complaint or that either equitable tolling or equitable estoppel applies. The relation back doctrine is not applicable since Mr. Juarez's Government Code claim shows that he knew about the City's possible liability before filing his complaint and thus Mr. Juarez cannot avail himself of the CCP 474 procedures. Equitable tolling is inapplicable since there was no prior lawsuit which tolls the running of any time period in this lawsuit. Equitable estoppel is inapplicable since the City's denial of the Government Code claim did not make any misleading statements. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
